topic.what: Data-free membership inference attack on FL segmentation models for hardware assurance
topic.how: Guides gradient inversion attack using publicly-available SCLLs as candidate labels; reconstruction fidelity serves as membership proxy; novel Ldummy loss term amplifies attack effectiveness for structurally complex data
topic.why_matters: Challenges FL's assumed privacy guarantees; enables inference of hardware metadata (node/layer) from intercepted model updates without any auxiliary data, posing severe IP leakage risk for semiconductor manufacturers

characteristics.what_it_measures: Membership inference attack success (diffusion layer member)
characteristics.evidence: Table II - near-perfect classification of diffusion layer members
characteristics.context: Inter-layer attack with λdummy=0; diffusion layer simpler structure is robust to post-processing

characteristics.what_it_measures: Membership inference attack success (metal layer member)
characteristics.evidence: Table III - optimal AUC achieved at λdummy=5
characteristics.context: Metal layer complex fine structures benefit from Ldummy term to overcome post-processing bottleneck

characteristics.what_it_measures: Intra-layer membership inference (32nm vs 90nm diffusion nodes)
characteristics.evidence: Table IV - 32nm member with λdummy=5 achieves near-perfect separation
characteristics.context: Finer 32nm features benefit most from conditional amplifier mechanism

characteristics.what_itasures: Dice similarity between reconstructed and target binary masks (diffusion member, diffusion guide)
characteristics.evidence: Table I - significantly higher reconstruction quality for true members
characteristics.context: Same-category guidance yields higher fidelity reconstruction
Near-perfect membership detection for diffusion layer

characteristics.evidence: Table II - baseline lower due to post-processing degradation of fine structures
characteristics.context: Performance gap explained by post-processing bottleneck for complex metal layer structures

characteristics.what_it_measures: FL communication rounds for segmentation model
characteristics.evidence: Section III-C - FedAvg with 2 local epochs per round
characteristics.context: Realistic FL setup with 2 clients, 50 images each

characteristics.evidence: Section III-A - AMD EPYC Rome CPU + NVIDIA B200 GPU
characteristics.context: Includes FL training, GIA, and MIA

good_for: assessing FL privacy leakage in semiconductor supply chain
detecting hardware metadata leakage from federated models
evaluating defense mechanisms against reconstruction-based attacks
testing organization-level IP exposure via model updates
not_for: real-time attack deployment (1hr compute per gradient)
requires: intercepted model updates from target client
public SCLLs from PDK (standard cell library layouts)
GPU compute for gradient inversion optimization (4000 iterations)

novelty: First data-free MIA on FL segmentation models using SCLLs as gradient inversion priors
Discovery of post-processing bottleneck that masks true leakage for complex structures
Conditional amplifier mechanism (negative Ldummy loss term) that widens reconstruction quality gap
Inference of hardware metadata (node/layer) not just individual data points
